content:
First, by the similarity $\triangle ABC\sim\triangle ACD$ with the correspondence $A\mapsto A$, $B\mapsto C$, $C\mapsto D$, the scale factor is 
$$r=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{6}{4}=\frac32.$$ 
Hence 
$$CD=r\cdot BC=\frac32\cdot5=7.5,\qquad DA=r\cdot CA=\frac32\cdot6=9.$$

Next place 
$$A=(0,0),\quad B=(4,0),\quad C\bigl(\tfrac{27}{8},\tfrac{15\sqrt7}{8}\bigr),$$ 
which indeed gives $AB=4,\ BC=5,\ CA=6.$  
By rotating and dilating the vector $AB=(4,0)$ by the angle $\angle CAB$ and factor $r$ one finds 
$$D=\bigl(9\cos2\alpha,\;9\sin2\alpha\bigr)
=\Bigl(-\tfrac{423}{128},\;\tfrac{405\sqrt7}{128}\Bigr),$$ 
where $\alpha=\angle CAB$ satisfies $\cos\alpha=\tfrac{9}{16},\ \sin\alpha=\tfrac{5\sqrt7}{16}.$  

We seek $P$ on the ray $AD$ beyond $A$, so set 
$$P=A+t\,(A-D)\,,\;t>0,\quad P=\bigl(-t\,D_x,\,-t\,D_y\bigr).$$  
One computes the two angles 
$$\angle BDC\quad\text{and}\quad\angle ACP$$ 
in coordinates, forms the equation 
$$\cos\angle BDC=\cos\angle ACP,$$ 
and after a somewhat lengthy but straightforward algebraic reduction one finds the unique positive solution 
$$t=\frac49.$$  
Finally 
$$DP^2=\bigl|\,P-D\bigr|^2
=\bigl|(t+1)\,D\,\bigr|^2
=(t+1)^2\cdot|D|^2
=(1+\tfrac49)^2\cdot9^2
=\frac{13^2}{9^2}\cdot81
=169.$$

Answer: $\boxed{169}$.
